{
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "THOUGHT: Now I'll create the build script according to the requirements. The script needs to:
1. Exclude specific patterns
2. Normalize permissions based on rules
3. Normalize line endings for text files
4. Set deterministic timestamps
5. Create a sorted, deterministic archive

Let me start by creating the basic structure of the build script:

```bash
cat <<'EOF' > /app/build.sh
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
SOURCE_DIR="/app/src"
OUTPUT_FILE="/app/release.tar.zst"
TEMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"

# Cleanup function
cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

# Copy source tree to temp directory for processing
rsync -a --delete "$SOURCE_DIR/" "$TEMP_DIR/"

# Remove excluded patterns
find "$TEMP_DIR" \( \
    -name ".git" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "node_modules" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "tmp" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name ".cache" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "__pycache__" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "*.tmp" -o \
    -name "*.pyc" -o \
    -name "*.o" \
\) -print0 | xargs -0 rm -rf

# Remove files with exactly 0600 permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -perm 600 -delete

# Normalize permissions
# Set directories to 0755
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

# Process files for permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    # Check if file has shebang or execute permission
    if [[ -x "$file" ]] || head -n 1 "$file" | grep -q "^#!"; then
        chmod 755 "$file"
    else
        chmod 644 "$file"
    fi
done

# Normalize line endings for text files
# First detect text files by mime type
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    if file --mime-type "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "text/"; then
        # Convert CRLF to LF
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
    fi
done

# Also normalize by common text file extensions
TEXT_EXTENSIONS="txt md py sh c h js css html xml json yaml yml"
for ext in $TEXT_EXTENSIONS; do
    find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -name "*.$ext" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
    done
done

# Set deterministic timestamps
if [ -z "${SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH:-}" ]; then
    TIMESTAMP=946684800  # 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
else
    TIMESTAMP="$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH"
fi

find "$TEMP_DIR" -print0 | xargs -0 touch -h -d "@$TIMESTAMP"

# Create deterministic archive
cd "$TEMP_DIR"
# Sort entries by name for determinism
find . -print0 | LC_ALL=C sort -z | \
    tar --null -T - --format=gnu --owner=0 --group=0 --mtime="@${TIMESTAMP}" -cf - | \
    zstd -19 --long --no-check --single-thread > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Build complete: $OUTPUT_FILE"
EOF
```",

\) -print0 | xargs -0 rm -rf

# Remove files with exactly 0600 permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -perm 600 -delete

# Normalize permissions

find "$TEMP_DIR" -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

# Process files for permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    # Check if file has shebang or execute permission
    if [[ -x "$file" ]] || head -n 1 "$file" | grep -q "^#!"; then
        chmod 755 "$file"
    else
        chmod 644 "$file"
    fi
done

# Normalize line endings for text files

find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    if file --mime-type "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "text/"; then
        # Convert CRLF to LF
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"

done

# Also normalize by common text file extensions

for ext in $TEXT_EXTENSIONS; do
    find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -name "*.$ext" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
    done
done

# Set deterministic timestamps
if [ -z "${SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH:-}" ]; then
    TIMESTAMP=946684800  # 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
else
    TIMESTAMP="$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH"

{
      "role": "user",
      "content": "<returncode>0</returncode>
<output>
#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
SOURCE_DIR="/app/src"
OUTPUT_FILE="/app/release.tar.zst"
TEMP_DIR="$(mktemp -d)"

# Cleanup function
cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

# Copy source tree to temp directory for processing
rsync -a --delete "$SOURCE_DIR/" "$TEMP_DIR/"

# Remove excluded patterns
find "$TEMP_DIR" \( \
    -name ".git" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "node_modules" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "tmp" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name ".cache" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "__pycache__" -type d -prune -exec rm -rf {} + -o \
    -name "*.tmp" -o \
    -name "*.pyc" -o \
    -name "*.o" \
\) -print0 | xargs -0 rm -rf

# Remove files with exactly 0600 permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -perm 600 -delete

# Normalize permissions
# Set directories to 0755
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

# Process files for permissions
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    # Check if file has shebang or execute permission
    if [[ -x "$file" ]] || head -n 1 "$file" | grep -q "^#!"; then
        chmod 755 "$file"
    else
        chmod 644 "$file"
    fi
done

# Normalize line endings for text files
# First detect text files by mime type
find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
    if file --mime-type "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "text/"; then
        # Convert CRLF to LF
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
    fi
done

# Also normalize by common text file extensions
TEXT_EXTENSIONS="txt md py sh c h js css html xml json yaml yml"
for ext in $TEXT_EXTENSIONS; do
    find "$TEMP_DIR" -type f -name "*.$ext" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' file; do
        sed -i 's/\r$//' "$file"
    done
done

# Set deterministic timestamps
if [ -z "${SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH:-}" ]; then
    TIMESTAMP=946684800  # 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC
else
    TIMESTAMP="$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH"
fi

find "$TEMP_DIR" -print0 | xargs -0 touch -h -d "@$TIMESTAMP"

# Create deterministic archive
cd "$TEMP_DIR"
# Sort entries by name for determinism
find . -print0 | LC_ALL=C sort -z | \
    tar --null -T - --format=gnu --owner=0 --group=0 --mtime="@${TIMESTAMP}" -cf - | \
    zstd -19 --long --no-check --single-thread > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Build complete: $OUTPUT_FILE"
</output>",
      "timestamp": 1766870078.988193
    },
